text: Technology and AI execution risk represents a material concern for Apple. According to recent analysis, Apple has fallen behind in the AI arms race, which has contributed to investor bearishness on the stock. While the broader market rallied from its lows in April and May 2025, Apple's stock remained relatively flat, down 15% for the year at the time of reporting. This underperformance suggests market participants are concerned about Apple's competitive positioning in artificial intelligence relative to peers. The company's R&D intensity of 9.43% in Q3 2025 indicates significant investment in innovation, though the market appears to question whether this spending is sufficient to close the AI gap.
